headline: Iran's New Supreme Leader
caption: Iran has named Mojtaba Khamenei, the son of slain Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as the country's new supreme leader. The 56-year-old was selected by Iran's Assembly of Experts just over a week after his father was killed in US and Israeli strikes on Tehran. Trump called the choice "unacceptable" and said he should have had a role in choosing Iran's next leader. Israel has vowed to target any successor.

headline: Epstein: Pam Bondi Subpoenaed
caption: The House Oversight Committee voted 24-19 to subpoena Attorney General Pam Bondi over the Epstein files, with five Republicans crossing the aisle to make it happen.

headline: Iran's Drone Strategy
caption: Iran’s strategy of launching large numbers of cheap Shahed drones is forcing the U.S. and its allies to accelerate efforts to develop far cheaper ways to shoot them down. “If they conclude that the West has burned through too many interceptors to defend itself, Russia might pursue aggressive action against NATO, or China could move against Taiwan.” — The Atlantic

headline: U.S. Tariffs Raised Again
caption: The Trump administration plans to raise global tariffs to 15% this week, up from the current 10%, According to Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, the the tariffs could return to the previous high levels within five months as the administration pursues alternative legal routes. The new tariff is being imposed temporarily for up to 150 days while the administration prepares more permanent trade measures.

headline: Trump to Start Tariff Refunds
caption: A federal trade judge ordered the Trump administration to begin building the refund process for overturned tariffs and outline how companies can reclaim billions. The administration is expected to appeal the decision as it moves to slow or potentially block the repayments.

headline: Poland's Nuclear Posture
caption: Polish PM Donald Tusk signaled this week that Warsaw intends to pursue its own nuclear capabilities as Europe scrambles to shore up its defense posture amid growing uncertainty over U.S. commitments to the continent.

headline: US/Iran War to Last 4-5 Weeks
caption: President Donald Trump said U.S. military operations against Iran are expected to last four to five weeks, though he said that he was prepared “to go far longer than that.” U.S. and Israeli airstrikes have already hit hundreds of targets across Iran, while Tehran has retaliated with missile and drone attacks across the region, including strikes near Gulf energy infrastructure and U.S. diplomatic sites.

headline: UK/France to Deploy to Iran
caption: “Britain and France, neither of which is part of the assault on Iran that began on Saturday, announced they would use their navies and air forces to help blunt Iran’s retaliatory strikes. Greece also sent planes and warships to its neighbor, Cyprus.” - The New York Times

headline: Kristi Noem Fired
caption:
Kristi Noem was removed today as Secretary of the Department of Homeland Security, with Trump replacing her as Oklahoma Republican Senator Markwayne Mullin.
However, Trump says he’ll be designating her as “Special Envoy for The Shield of the Americas,” his new security initiative in which he would be focusing on the Western Hemisphere.

headline: U.S./Ecuador Team Up
caption: The United States and Ecuador are preparing joint operations targeting powerful drug gangs behind a surge of violence across the country. U.S. forces have been deployed, but will NOT participate in the missions, and instead only provide intelligence, surveillance and logistical support while Ecuadorian forces carry out the raids tied to cocaine shipments moving through Ecuador’s ports.

headline: Iran Shuts Off Strait of Hormuz
caption: The war between the U.S., Israel and Iran has severely disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global trade route, leaving thousands of oil tankers and cargo vessels stranded. Iran says it has shut the narrow waterway and warned it will attack any ships attempting to pass through it.

headline: Senate Votes Against Ending Iran War
caption: Senate Republicans blocked an effort to halt Trump’s war with Iran, effectively backing the early stages of a conflict that has spread quickly across the Middle East while leaving the United States without a clear path for disengagement.

headline: France Expands Nuclear Arsenal
caption:
“Macron’s plan expands France’s nuclear arsenal, ends public disclosure of total stockpile numbers, permits temporary forward-basing of nuclear forces in allied European states, and deepens deterrence cooperation with partners.”
— Atlantic Council

headline: U.S. Sinks Iran Warship & NATO Strikes Missile Headed For Turkey
caption: A U.S. submarine sank an Iranian warship near Sri Lanka, killing at least 80 people, while NATO air defenses intercepted and destroyed an Iranian ballistic missile launched toward Turkey.

headline: Talarico Wins Texas Democratic Nomination
caption: Texas state Rep. James Talarico has won the Democratic nomination for U.S. Senate in Texas. He will face the Republican nominee in November in a race that could become one of the most closely watched Senate contests of 2026.
